rulings.Case Title: TINOCO, ELVIRA AS WRONGFUL DEATH HEAR AND et al VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Apportion Proceeding And For Order To Distribute Settlement Funds
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 19, 2016 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiffs' motion for apportionment proceeding and for order to distribute settlement funds: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30a.m. Brayton Purcell must bring a proposed order to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN BROSNAN VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 19, 2016 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issues 1 through 4 (negligent and strict liability design defect and failure to warn) and granted as to Issue 5 (punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony (as to the flange gaskets contained in the boxes with the Crane valves) and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Even if defendant did sustain its initial burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and the deposition testimony of William McLean and Anthony Pantaleoni create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (flange gaskets and internal bonnet gasket) attributable to defendant. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his increased risk of developing his asbestos-related illness. Plaintiff's responses to defendant's special discovery preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to Issues 1 through 4 for the same reasons stated above. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication is granted as to Issue 5 because defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiff failed to submit clear and convincing evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: LOUIS REYES VS. GENUINE PARTS COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 19, 2016 in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant ArvinMeritor, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs' claims are time-barred pursuant to C.C.P. § 340.2. However even if defendant did sustain its initial burden, the deposition testimony and declaration of Mr. Reyes would create a triable issue whether plaintiffs' claims are time-barred. Defendant sustained its burden of demonstrating that exposure to asbestos did not cause or contribute to Mr. Reyes' colon cancer. The declaration of Dr. Richard Cohen creates a triable issue whether exposure to asbestos caused or contributed to Mr. Reyes' colon cancer. Defendant's request for an Evidence Code § 402 hearing is denied. Plaintiffs' objections to defendant's Exhibits N, O and P are overruled. Plaintiffs' objections to the deposition testimony of Dr. Cohen submitted with defendant's reply are overruled. Defendant's objections to the declarations of Mr. Reyes and Dr. Cohen are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
